content: Just as the high school English literacy framework teaches students to turn 'reflecting on written work' from a passive vocabulary definition into an active, iterative critical thinking practice, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this pedagogical structure to move beyond single-perspective error correction and train agents to refine their outputs through guided, discipline-aligned reflective practice. This framing turns LLM self-reflection from a niche technical hack into a replicable skill-building process parallel to human learning.
